tasks.description: Create research module scaffold and OHLCV history fetcher with timestamps and pagination support.
acceptance_criteria: research/__init__.py exists as module root.
research/ohlcv_history.py provides fetch_ohlcv_history(pair, interval, since) returning a DataFrame with timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume columns.
Timestamps are Unix epoch integers in the DataFrame index or column.
Pagination support: fetches multiple pages via Kraken since parameter to cover arbitrary date ranges.
Reuses exchange/ohlcv.py's kraken_pair_name for pair normalization.
Tests cover single-page fetch, pagination, and error handling with mocked HTTP.

tasks.description: Create SQLite research reader for offline dataset export from fills, orders, and closed positions.
acceptance_criteria: research/db_reader.py provides ResearchReader class that reads from an existing SQLite database.
Methods: fetch_fills() returns DataFrame with pair, side, quantity, price, fee, filled_at columns.
Methods: fetch_orders() returns DataFrame with order_id, pair, position_id, exchange_order_id, client_order_id, recorded_fee, created_at.
Methods: fetch_closed_trades() returns DataFrame with position_id, pair, created_at, closed_at.
Uses persistence/sqlite.py's open_database for connection management.
Tests use an in-memory SQLite database with known test data.

tasks.description: Implement forward-looking label computation from OHLCV data.
acceptance_criteria: research/labels.py provides compute_labels(ohlcv_df, horizons=[6,12]) returning a DataFrame.
Label columns: return_sign_6h, return_sign_12h, return_bps_6h, return_bps_12h, regime_label.
return_sign is 1 for positive, -1 for negative, 0 for zero return.
return_bps is the return in basis points (100 * pct_return).
regime_label is derived from realized volatility: low/medium/high.
Labels at row t use only close prices at t+horizon — this is the forward-looking ground truth, not a feature.
Rows where the horizon extends beyond available data have NaN labels.
Tests verify label correctness on synthetic OHLCV data and NaN handling at boundaries.

tasks.description: Build dataset builder that assembles market features and labels into versioned Parquet files.
acceptance_criteria: research/dataset_builder.py provides DatasetBuilder class.
build_dataset(pair, interval, since, until) method orchestrates OHLCV fetch, DB read, label computation.
Features DataFrame includes only point-in-time data: OHLCV columns plus any trade-derived features available at time t.
No feature uses future data — features at row t use only data at or before t.
Outputs market_v1.parquet and labels_v1.parquet to a configurable output directory.
Outputs manifest_v1.json with schema version, pair, interval, row count, date range, and generation timestamp.
Tests verify Parquet output structure, manifest content, and deterministic output for identical inputs.

tasks.description: Add CLI entrypoint for running the dataset export.
acceptance_criteria: research/cli.py is runnable as python -m research.cli or python research/cli.py.
Accepts --pair, --interval, --since, --output-dir arguments.
Defaults: pair=DOGE/USD, interval=60, output-dir=data/research.
Prints summary of exported dataset (row count, date range, output paths).
Test verifies CLI argument parsing and that main() calls DatasetBuilder correctly.

tasks.description: Add integration test for determinism and point-in-time correctness.
acceptance_criteria: tests/research/test_integration.py runs a full build_dataset cycle on synthetic data.
Verifies that running the builder twice on identical input produces byte-identical Parquet files.
Verifies that no label column value at row t depends on OHLCV data after row t (point-in-time test).
Verifies manifest_v1.json contains expected metadata fields.
All existing tests still pass.
